heading: Preference eligibles; promotion; physical qualifications; waiver
label: 3363 Preference eligibles; promotion; physical qualifications; waiver
notes: Historical and Revision NotesDerivationU.S. CodeRevised Statutes andStatutes at Large (1st 2 sentences, so much as relates to promotion).5 U.S.C. 854(1st 2 sentences, so much as relates to promotion), .June 27, 1944, ch. 287, § 558 Stat. 388The section is restated to conform to section 3312.The words “in the competitive service, an Executive agency, or the government of the District of Columbia” are added on authority of former sections 851, 858, and 869, which are carried into this title. The last sentence is added on authority of former section 869.Standard changes are made to conform with the definitions applicable and the style of this title as outlined in the preface to the report.

Amendments1978— substituted “Office of Personnel Management” and “Office” for “Civil Service Commission” and “Commission”, respectively.Pub. L. 95–4541975— struck out “, except an appointment made under ” after “or made with the advice and consent of, the Senate”.Pub. L. 94–183section 3311 of title 39

children.text: requirements as to age, height, and weight, unless the requirement is essential to the performance of the duties of the position; and

physical requirements if, in the opinion of the Office or other examining agency, after considering the recommendation of an accredited physician, the preference eligible is physically able to perform efficiently the duties of the position.
